
---
title: Programming Languages and - Why Julia?
---

::: {.callout-tip}
# Installation

Make sure to check out the [installation](/installation.qmd) page on this website before you come to class.

:::


## Basics: REPL, VSCode, Notebooks

We will repeat the basics of julia in a very condensed manner by going through steps 1-7 of [MoJuWo](https://modernjuliaworkflows.org) live in class.

julia REPL:

![This is the julia REPL](/assets/REPL.png)


VScode:

![This is VSCode](/assets/VSCode.png)

Notice how there is a julia REPL running _as part of_ the VSCode editor. This is called an _integrated development environment_ (IDE). Very similar to an `R` session connected to `RStudio`, or the `python` interpreter being run through `PyCharm` etc.



## Notebooks

We use several Pluto.jl notebooks in this lecture. 

Topic | rendered Notebook | source of notebook
:-----: | :--------: | :-------:
Variables | [click](https://pluto.land/n/8lkdfpkp) | [click](https://github.com/floswald/CompEcon/raw/refs/heads/main/notebooks/week1/01-variables.jl)
Functions | [click](https://pluto.land/n/8upgr929) | [click](https://github.com/floswald/CompEcon/raw/refs/heads/main/notebooks/week1/02-functions.jl)
Julia is 🏎️ fast | [click](https://pluto.land/n/bfqj5h7n) | [click](https://github.com/floswald/CompEcon/raw/refs/heads/main/notebooks/week1/00-fast.jl)

Notice for the last one you need a suitable installation of C and fortran compilers on your system installed.

### Resources

* [Modern Julia Workflow](https://modernjuliaworkflows.org)
* [StartHere.jl](https://github.com/JuliaCommunity/StartHere.jl)
* [Aruoba and Fernandez-Villaverde](https://www.sas.upenn.edu/~jesusfv/comparison_languages.pdf)
* [Jesus' Julia Tutorial](https://www.sas.upenn.edu/~jesusfv/Chapter_HPC_8_Julia.pdf)
* [Chapter 1 of Julia for Data Analysis](https://www.manning.com/books/julia-for-data-analysis)
